rationale: The citing paper uses Paharia et al. (2014) as a key prior result from the underdog-brand literature to justify why consumers may support smaller, disadvantaged crowdfunded firms. This citation appears in the theory development (not just the reference list), where it helps frame the inequality/underdog logic behind consumer preference for crowdfunded products. The paper also revisits this link in the discussion, claiming crowdfunding can serve as a new signal of underdog status. Although the cited work is not methodologically reused, it meaningfully informs conceptual positioning.
relation_description: The citing paper builds its conceptual framing on prior underdog-support findings from Paharia et al. (2014), using them to argue that crowdfunded firms may receive consumer support as disadvantaged players. It then positions crowdfunding as an additional signal-based route to underdog preference.
